title: corethrogyne filaginifolia
intro: Corethrogyne filaginifolia, also known as Bigelow's woollyhead, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae, native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Corethrogyne filaginifolia include its potential to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe skin irritations, making it a valuable herb for various health concerns. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, allowing for diverse applications in traditional medicine.
